title: Exotic, Smoky Roasted Greek goat meat Pastitsio
description: A rich and hearty Greek dish featuring goat meat, roasted to perfection and served with a creamy bechamel sauce.

ingredients.name: 1.5 lbs goat meat
ingredients.notes: lean goat shoulder or leg, boneless

ingredients.name: 2 cups mixed mushrooms
ingredients.notes: such as cremini, shiitake and button

ingredients.name: 1 cup goat milk
ingredients.notes: or substitute with lacto-vegetarian milk alternative

ingredients.name: 1 cup grated kefalograviera cheese
ingredients.notes: or substitute with other hard, salty cheese

instructions: Season the goat meat with cumin, smoked paprika, salt and pepper.
Grill the goat meat over high heat, yakimono-style, until browned on all sides.
Sear the mushrooms and onion in a pan over medium heat until tender.
Transfer the goat meat to a tava-griddle or large cast-iron skillet.
Add the mushrooms, onion and garlic to the tava-griddle and stir to combine.
Transfer the tava-griddle to the preheated oven and roast for 20-25 minutes, or until the goat meat is cooked through.
While the goat meat is roasting, prepare the bechamel sauce by melting 2 tbsp of butter in a saucepan over medium heat.
Whisk in 1 cup of goat milk and bring the mixture to a simmer.
Reduce heat to low and let the sauce simmer for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally.
When the goat meat is done, remove it from the oven and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.

To assemble the pastitsio, place a layer of cooked pasta in the bottom of a baking dish, followed by a layer of sliced goat meat, a layer of bechamel sauce and finally a layer of grated kefalograviera cheese.
Repeat the layering process until all ingredients are used up, finishing with a layer of cheese on top.
Transfer the pastitsio to the oven and bake at 375°F (190°C) for 20-25 minutes, or until the cheese is melted and bubbly.
